identitySentence: Hammerle Morris Law Firm has served Denton County families and businesses since 1984 from its Lewisville office, guiding clients through estate planning, probate, guardianship, elder law, and civil litigation.

concept.headline: Planning for your family, your business, and what comes next — start the conversation with Hammerle Morris.
concept.subheadline: Whether you are settling a loved one's estate, protecting an aging parent, or planning ahead for your own family, you probably have more questions than answers right now. A short consultation with a Denton County firm that has done this work since 1984 can turn those questions into a clear next step.
credibilityPoints: Founding attorney Virginia Hammerle, Board Certified in Civil Trial Law, practicing since 1982
Attorneys with Texas bar admissions reaching back to 1977
AV-rated by Martindale-Hubbell, with offices in Lewisville, Denton, and Plano

sections.title: Practice focus
sections.body: The firm's attorneys handle estate planning, probate, guardianship, and elder law alongside business law, banking law, and civil litigation. That combination matters when a family matter touches a business, a piece of real estate, or a dispute that needs a steady hand.

sections.title: Denton roots
sections.body: Headquartered in Lewisville since 1984 with a satellite office in Denton, the firm has spent four decades working with Denton County families, and its attorneys are members of the Denton County Bar Association.

sections.title: What to expect
sections.body: Tell us briefly what brings you in, and the firm will follow up to set a consultation time that works for you. Come ready to describe your situation honestly — the first conversation is about understanding your matter, not judging it.

outreachDraft.subject: Proof matters more than promises: 15 days, 1 demo, and a Lead Capturer that can pay for itself before you ever have to.
outreachDraft.body:
DRAFT — NOT SENT. Review and authorize before any outreach.

To the team at Hammerle Morris Law Firm,

Your firm has been serving Denton County since 1984 — longer than most practices in the county have existed — with founding attorney Virginia Hammerle Board Certified in Civil Trial Law and attorneys whose Texas bar admissions reach back to 1977. That kind of history earns trust. We noticed something much more recent, though: when someone finds your site tonight with a probate or guardianship question, the only paths to you are a contact form and a phone call during office hours.

Your website is genuinely strong — clear practice pages, detailed attorney bios, abundant trust signals. The one gap is what happens after a visitor fills out the form: they wait, often anxiously, sometimes contacting the next firm on their list while they wait. For a practice built on estate planning, probate, guardianship, and elder law — matters where people reach out worried, grieving, or planning under time pressure — that silence costs consultations you never know you lost.

That is exactly what our Lead Capturer fixes, and only that. We built one specifically for Hammerle Morris. It carries your identity sentence: "Hammerle Morris Law Firm has served Denton County families and businesses since 1984 from its Lewisville office, guiding clients through estate planning, probate, guardianship, elder law, and civil litigation." It includes a custom contact form written for your practice areas, so a visitor with a probate question and a visitor with an employment dispute are handled differently from the first keystroke.

Here is how it works:

1. Every inquiry is emailed directly to your firm's inbox — no dashboard to learn, no middleman holding your leads.
2. The potential client automatically receives a topical response specific to what they wrote about, in your firm's voice.
3. That response presents your available consultation times so they can pick one while their need is urgent.
4. A $50 reservation deposit confirms the appointment, so your calendar fills with committed consultations instead of no-shows.

We are not asking you to take any of this on faith. We are offering a 15-day proof period with the complete experience live for your firm. Test it end to end, submit inquiries yourself, watch every email arrive. If it does not perform, you walk away having spent nothing but fifteen minutes.

How to test your Lead Capturer (15 minutes):

1. Submit a clearly labeled TEST inquiry using a customer email address you control.
2. Do not enter confidential client information anywhere in the test.
3. Confirm the firm inbox receives a real email.
4. Confirm the notification contains every displayed form field.
5. Confirm the subject line identifies the firm and the likely matter type.
6. Confirm the Reply-To address contains the submitted customer email.
7. Confirm the system automatically generates a response specific to the TEST inquiry.
8. Confirm that response is sent to the customer email without any staff action.
9. Confirm the customer email actually receives it.
10. Confirm the response addresses the actual topic of the inquiry.
11. Confirm genuine available consultation times are presented.
12. Select a time.
13. Confirm the $50 reservation requirement appears.
14. Confirm the preparation list fits the matter type.
15. Confirm the prohibited-item list includes legal documents.
16. In a payment sandbox, confirm the appointment locks only after a verified $50 payment event.

If any step fails, the test fails — contact us and we will make it right before you owe anything.

Fifteen days. One demo. Your own inbox as the scoreboard.

Respectfully,
The IntakeBrief Team

[Physical mailing address: provided before any send]

If you would prefer not to hear from us again, reply with 'unsubscribe' and we will remove you immediately.
